---
title: "LeetCode 1051. Height Checker"
subtitle: "Height Checker"
lang: en
date: 2026-07-16
author: Anthony
categories: [LeetCode, Array]
description: "[Problem link](https://leetcode.com/problems/height-checker/description/)"
image: ../../cover/LeetCode_Logo_black_with_text.svg
alternate-lang: "/dsa/posts/leetcode/1051-height-checker/1051-height-checker.html"
---

A school is taking its annual photograph, and students are required to line up in non-decreasing order of height — from shortest to tallest, with students of equal height standing next to each other.

Given an array `heights` representing the heights of the students as they are currently standing, count and return how many students are not standing in the correct position — in other words, the minimum number of students who would need to move for the line to be in the correct order.

For example, given

```
heights = [1, 1, 4, 2, 1, 3]
```

the correct order would be `[1, 1, 1, 2, 3, 4]`. In the given array, the values at indices `2`, `3`, and `5` (namely `4`, `2`, and the last `3`) are out of place, so `3` students are standing incorrectly, and the answer is `3`.

## Approach

The most natural approach is to copy the original array, sort the copy to obtain the correct order, and then compare it element by element against the original array, counting every mismatch along the way.

## Implementation

::: {.panel-tabset}
## C
```c
// Ascending comparator
int cmp(const void *a, const void *b)
{
    return *(int *)a - *(int *)b;
}

int heightChecker(int *heights, int heightsSize)
{
    int counter = 0;

    // Copy and sort to get expected order
    int *tmp = (int *)malloc(heightsSize * sizeof(int));
    memcpy(tmp, heights, heightsSize * sizeof(int));
    qsort(tmp, heightsSize, sizeof(int), cmp);

    // Compare with original
    for (int i = 0; i < heightsSize; i++)
    {
        if (heights[i] != tmp[i])
        {
            counter++;
        }
    }

    free(tmp);
    return counter;
}
```

## C++
```cpp
int heightChecker(std::vector<int> &heights)
{
    int counter = 0;

    // Copy and sort to get expected order
    vector<int> tmp = heights;
    sort(tmp.begin(), tmp.end());

    // Compare with original
    for (size_t i = 0; i < heights.size(); i++)
    {
        if (heights[i] != tmp[i])
        {
            counter++;
        }
    }

    return counter;
}
```

## Python

```python
def heightChecker(self, heights: list[int]) -> int:
    counter = 0

    # Sort a copy to get expected order
    tmp = sorted(heights)

    # Compare with original
    for i in range(len(heights)):
        if heights[i] != tmp[i]:
            counter += 1

    return counter
```
:::
